subject: Small thing — external emails not arriving — internal email works fine
description:
Hey team, just a small thing. I want to start by saying this might be user error, I'm not great with technology, but here goes nothing — External emails haven't been arriving for the past few hours. I know because a vendor called to ask why I didn't reply to their urgent email. My internal email works perfectly. Nothing in Junk, nothing in Quarantine that I can see.

Just to give you full context, I've been at Contoso for 8 years and this is maybe the third or fourth time I've had to submit a ticket. Usually things just work which is great. But this time I'm really stuck and my manager is asking about the deliverables that I can't complete because of this issue.

By the way, I noticed the same issue showing up on the monitoring dashboard for the production database. Probably nothing but wanted to mention it.

Also — my Teams background effects have stopped working. Not a big deal but it's annoying for client calls when they can see my messy home office.

subject: SAP license count exceeded — users getting kicked out

This is causing data entry errors and lost work. We're in the middle of quarterly close.

Just to give you full context, I've been at Contoso for 8 years and this is maybe the third or fourth time I've had to submit a ticket. Usually things just work which is great. subject: Minor issue — sql injection vulnerability discovered in the reporting dashboard
description:
Not a big deal but thought I'd report it. During a routine code review of our internal client reporting portal, I found what appears to be a SQL injection vulnerability in the account lookup endpoint (/api/v2/accounts/search). The query parameter 'accountId' is being concatenated directly into the SQL query without parameterization. This portal has access to the client database with PII and financial data. I haven't tested it with a live exploit but the code pattern is clearly vulnerable.

Oh, also, I think this might be affecting the other 40 people on the trading floor too, but I haven't confirmed. The trade execution system seems a bit slow as well.

The projector in our main conference room has stopped working. It tries to turn on but shuts off after a few seconds. We've got a big client presentation this week and there's no other room with a large enough display. Can someone fix or replace it ASAP?

The CEO needs this resolved before the 3pm investor meeting. Please escalate immediately.

subject: Databricks interactive cluster stuck in pending state (+ another issue)
description:
Can't launch my Databricks cluster. I've tried three times and it keeps going to a 'Terminated' state after about 5 minutes. The event log shows 'The requested VM size Standard_DS4_v2 is not available in the current region.' This is the cluster we use for all our data processing. Need this fixed urgently.

Separately, I also need my VPN client updated. It says version 3.1 but I was told we're supposed to be on 3.4. Can you handle that too?

subject: How do I book a meeting room? (+ another issue)
description:
Quick question — what's the process for reserving a conference room? I tried searching in Outlook but none of the room lists show up. Is there a specific tool or portal we use for room bookings?

Separately, I also need my VPN client updated. It says version 3.1 but I was told we're supposed to be on 3.4. Can you handle that too?

subject: Fwd: Re: Can't complete MFA — phone shows no notifications
description:
---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: helpdesk@contoso.com
Date: Yesterday at 4:30 PM
Subject: Re: Can't complete MFA — phone shows no notifications
To: reporter@contoso.com

Hi, we're looking into this. Can you provide more details?

---

On Mon, reporter@contoso.com wrote:

> Got a new phone and set up the Authenticator app but MFA prompts aren't coming through. I re-registered in the app but it still doesn't push. I can log in using the 6-digit code but it's really inconvenient.

---

Adding more context: the issue is still happening. Nothing has changed since my original report. Please prioritize.

subject: Re: Re: Re: Specific apps seem to be bandwidth-throttled
description:
Latest update — still not resolved.

On Tuesday, helpdesk@contoso.com wrote:
> We've escalated this to the engineering team.
> They should reach out within 24 hours.

On Monday, I wrote:
> Downloads from our Azure Blob Storage are extremely slow — topping out at about 2 Mbps when I should be getting much more. Regular internet browsing and speed tests show normal speeds (200+ Mbps). A couple of people on the data analytics team have noticed the same thing. We're trying to pull large datasets for the quarterly risk model refresh and it's taking hours instead of minutes. Feels like something is rate-limiting our Azure traffic.

It's been 48 hours and no one has contacted me.

subject: CRITICAL: Alert from managed detection service — active threat detected
description:
First of all, thank you for all the great work the IT team does. I really appreciate it. Now, about my issue — Our managed detection and response (MDR) provider CrowdStrike just escalated a critical alert. They've detected command-and-control (C2) beaconing traffic from three internal workstations to a known threat actor infrastructure at 185.220.101.42:443. The beaconing pattern is consistent with Cobalt Strike. The affected machines are in the 10.50.20.0/24 subnet which is our trading floor network. CrowdStrike recommends immediate isolation of the affected hosts. Their incident reference is CS-INC-2024-7823.

Just to give you full context, I've been at Contoso for 8 years and this is maybe the third or fourth time I've had to submit a ticket. Usually things just work which is great. But this time I'm really stuck and my manager is asking about the deliverables that I can't complete because of this issue.

A colleague in the Compliance team received a call from someone saying they were from Microsoft support and that our company's Microsoft 365 tenant was being attacked. They asked her to install a remote desktop tool so they could 'fix' her machine. She didn't install anything but she's worried because she confirmed her full name and email address.

This is for the CFO's direct report. They're preparing for the quarterly earnings call tomorrow morning. Please treat as highest priority.

subject: GlobalProtect won't connect on hotel WiFi

My phone hotspot works fine for VPN, so it seems like the hotel network is blocking something.

subject: STILL BROKEN — Unauthorized third-party app granted access to user mailboxes
description:
Follow-up to INC-3008 from last week. The issue was 'fixed' on Thursday but returned on Monday morning. Same symptoms:

Entra ID protection flagged a risky consent grant — a third-party OAuth application called 'SecureMailBackup Pro' was granted Mail.Read, Mail.ReadWrite, and Files.ReadWrite.All permissions by 8 users in the trading division this morning. The app publisher is unverified and the app ID (a3f7c912-4d8e-4b2a-9e1f-6c3d5a8b7e9f) doesn't match any approved applications. This looks like a consent phishing attack giving the attacker read/write access to mailboxes and OneDrive files.

At this point I'd like this escalated to a senior engineer.

subject: SQL injection vulnerability discovered in the reporting dashboard
description:
OK so this is going to be a long one, bear with me. It all started about two weeks ago when I noticed some weird behavior but I didn't report it because I thought it would fix itself. Well, it didn't. Here's what happened: Our application security scanner flagged a reflected XSS vulnerability in the internal performance reporting dashboard at https://reports.contoso.internal/dashboard. The 'dateRange' parameter in the URL is rendered without sanitization. While this is an internal app, it's accessible to all employees and displays client financial data.

Also, unrelated, but the coffee machine on Floor 3 is broken again. Not sure who handles that but figured I'd mention it since I'm already submitting a ticket.

Thanks for your help!

subject: Minor issue — cross-site scripting vulnerability in internal web application (+ another issue)
description:
Not a big deal but thought I'd report it. During a routine code review of our internal client reporting portal, I found what appears to be a SQL injection vulnerability in the account lookup endpoint (/api/v2/accounts/search). The query parameter 'accountId' is being concatenated directly into the SQL query without parameterization. This portal has access to the client database with PII and financial data. I haven't tested it with a live exploit but the code pattern is clearly vulnerable.

While I have your attention — my monitor also started flickering yesterday. Not sure if it's the cable or the monitor itself. It's a Dell U2722D.
